title: aquilegia bauhinii subsp. bertolonii
intro: Aquilegia bauhinii subsp. bertolonii is a rare and lesser-known species of the aquilegia plant, also known as the alpine columbine, which belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aquilegia bauhinii subsp. bertolonii include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions, and its roots are also used to make a medicinal syrup, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
